description: Comprehensive regression tests for mode switching, write restrictions, bash guards, file classification, mode state persistence, and implicit mode detection rules.

assertions.context: Default mode (no mode active) blocks writes to CODE files
Default mode blocks writes to SPEC files
Default mode blocks writes to INVARIANT files
Engineer mode allows writing source code files
Engineer mode allows writing .impl.md companion files
Engineer mode blocks writes to feature spec files
Engineer mode blocks writes to invariant files
PM mode allows writing feature spec files
PM mode blocks writes to source code files
PM mode blocks writes to .impl.md companion files
QA mode allows writing .discoveries.md files
QA mode blocks writes to code files
QA mode blocks writes to spec files
Default mode blocks rm commands
Default mode blocks git add commands
Default mode blocks git commit commands
Default mode blocks shell output redirects
Default mode allows read-only ls command
Default mode allows read-only git status
Default mode allows read-only git diff
Engineer mode allows all bash commands including rm
Engineer mode allows git commit
QA mode allows all bash commands

assertions.pattern: set_mode engineer then get_mode returns engineer
assertions.context: Mode state persists to disk and can be read back
Setting mode to None clears the active mode
PID-scoped mode files isolate concurrent sessions
Different sessions have independent mode state

assertions.pattern: session A still engineer after B changed
assertions.context: Changing one session's mode does not affect another
Empty PID-scoped file returns none, does not fall back to stale unscoped

assertions.pattern: no session ID falls back to unscoped file
assertions.context: Without SESSION_ID, get_mode reads unscoped current_mode
Files in features/_invariants/i_*.md classified as INVARIANT
Files ending in .discoveries.md classified as QA
Feature spec .md files classified as SPEC
Companion .impl.md files classified as CODE (Engineer-writable)
Python source files classified as CODE
Agent definition classified as CODE
Skill SKILL.md files classified as CODE
Agent definition opening statement uses forceful MANDATORY language
Agent definition forbids writes without active mode
Section 4.4 says activate mode directly, don't ask

assertions.pattern: section 4.4 says Call purlin_mode MCP tool
assertions.context: Section 4.4 instructs to call purlin_mode MCP tool

assertions.pattern: section 4.4 does NOT say invoke skill
assertions.context: Section 4.4 does not redirect to full skill invocation

assertions.pattern: mode guard uses Open mode label
assertions.context: Mode guard uses terse Open mode language, not soft default mode

assertions.pattern: mode guard does NOT say can read and research
assertions.context: Mode guard does not include comfort language about reading
Mode activation priority chain includes config default_mode
